title: rottboellia incurvata
intro: Rottboellia incurvata, also known as Black Acacia or Makabuhay, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. Rottboellia incurvata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling its roots and leaves in water, which is then used to treat various ailments.
